verdict: over-dispersion + secondary cascade explains it (catalog N-test passes where Poisson fails)
framing: ETAS branching forward-simulation of the in-window M>=mc count. The frozen-intensity forecast (64.3) omits within-window secondary triggering; the full cascade lifts the mean to ~82 and the over-dispersion (Fano~1.8) widens p95 to ~103, so the observed 96 is consistent. The Poisson N-test (assumes Var=mean) over-rejects it as a gross miss; the catalog-based test does not. This is one 30-day global window; the method generalises.
